verbatim_text: But later on, as you spend more training compute, still the accuracy is fairly limited. You don't see some problems quickly, but on other problems there is limited improvement. But if you use our set of rewards, then what we see during training is that there's a huge gain in accuracy on the training problems where previously exploration was a bottleneck. And in fact, this is one of the first results, or at least a major result, on scaling off-policy RL for RLs, where you're not directly supervising. By off-policy, I mean it's offline data. So typically we had online traces. It's offline data. So that's why it's off-policy. So you're not using it for supervision. You're using it to bootstrap online exploration. OK. So with that, we conclude the sampler part. Here the main idea was you want to reduce the burden of online exploration using the right choice of auxiliary data. That figures out how we sample a trace.
